key_insight: ComBench shows that Olympiad-level combinatorics is not one skill: rigorous proof reasoning and constructive realization behave differently across frontier models.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark helps diagnose where math-focused AI systems fail, guiding model improvements for proof fidelity, constructive search, and reliable evaluation.
method: Two task settings: analysis-centric proof problems and construction-centric existence/realization problems
Rubric-guided proof grading plus deterministic construction verification
evidence_signal: The strongest model reaches 65.4% overall Avg. and 75.3% overall Best@4, and Kimi-K2.6 can underperform GPT-5.5 on proof grading while outperforming it on construction tasks.
watch_item: Results may be sensitive to rubric quality and verifier design, especially for borderline proofs or constructions that are correct but hard to automatically validate.
paper_title: ComBench: A Benchmark for Rigorous Proof Reasoning and Constructive Realization in Olympiad-Level Combinatorics
